ṣaṭkōṇamadhyanilayaḥ ṣaṇḍatvaparihārakaḥ ।
sēnānīḥ subhagaḥ skandaḥ surānandaḥ satāṃ gatiḥ ॥ 115 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Shatkona-madhya-nilaya (dwelling at the centre of the six-pointed star), Shandatva-parihara-ka (remover of the condition of sterility, granter of progeny), Senanin (the great general, commander), Subhaga (the auspicious and fortunate), Skanda (the leaper, the one poured out), Surananda (joy of the gods), Satam-gati (the path and refuge for the righteous). (115)

contentBlocks.original:
सुब्रह्मण्यः सुराध्यक्षः सर्वज्ञः सर्वदः सुखी ।
सुलभः सिद्धिदः सौम्यः सिद्धेशः सिद्धिसाधनः ॥ 116 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
subrahmaṇyaḥ surādhyakṣaḥ sarvajñaḥ sarvadaḥ sukhī ।
sulabhaḥ siddhidaḥ saumyaḥ siddhēśaḥ siddhisādhanaḥ ॥ 116 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Subrahmanya (the dear one of the Brahmins, auspicious Brahman), Sur-adhyaksha (superintendent of the gods), Sarvajna (omniscient), Sarva-da (giver of all), Sukhi (the blissful one), Sulabha (easily accessible to devotees), Siddhi-da (bestower of perfections), Saumya (the gentle and gracious), Siddh-esha (lord of all perfections), Siddhi-sadhana (the means by which perfection is attained). (116)

contentBlocks.original:
सिद्धार्थः सिद्धसङ्कल्पः सिद्धसाधुः सुरेश्वरः ।
सुभुजः सर्वदृक्साक्षी सुप्रसादः सनातनः ॥ 117 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
siddhārthaḥ siddhasaṅkalpaḥ siddhasādhuḥ surēśvaraḥ ।
subhujaḥ sarvadṛksākṣī suprasādaḥ sanātanaḥ ॥ 117 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Siddh-artha (of accomplished purpose), Siddha-sankalpa (whose resolves are ever fulfilled), Siddha-sadhu (righteous among the perfected), Suresh-vara (lord of the gods), Subhuja (of beautiful and auspicious arms), Sarva-drik-sakshi (witness and seer of all), Suprasada (of gracious and easily-won grace), Sanatana (the eternal one). (117)

contentBlocks.original:
सुधापतिः स्वयञ्ज्योतिः स्वयम्भूः सर्वतोमुखः ।
समर्थः सत्कृतिः सूक्ष्मः सुघोषः सुखदः सुहृत् ॥ 118 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sudhāpatiḥ svayañjyōtiḥ svayambhūḥ sarvatōmukhaḥ ।
samarthaḥ satkṛtiḥ sūkṣmaḥ sughōṣaḥ sukhadaḥ suhṛt ॥ 118 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sudha-pati (lord of the nectar of immortality), Svayam-jyoti (self-luminous), Svayambhu (self-existent, self-born), Sarvatomukha (facing all directions), Samartha (supremely capable), Sat-kriti (of true and righteous deeds), Sukshma (the subtlest one), Sughosha (of sweet and auspicious sound), Sukha-da (giver of joy), Suhrit (the true friend of all). (118)

contentBlocks.original:
सुप्रसन्नः सुरश्रेष्ठः सुशीलः सत्यसाधकः ।
सम्भाव्यः सुमनाः सेव्यः सकलागमपारगः ॥ 119 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suprasannaḥ suraśrēṣṭhaḥ suśīlaḥ satyasādhakaḥ ।
sambhāvyaḥ sumanāḥ sēvyaḥ sakalāgamapāragaḥ ॥ 119 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Suprasanna (ever fully gracious), Sura-shreshtha (the best among the gods), Sushila (of most excellent conduct and character), Satya-sadhaka (achiever of truth), Sambhavya (worthy of highest reverence), Sumanas (of beautiful, pure mind), Sevya (worthy of service and worship), Sakal-agama-paraga (proficient in all the Agamas). (119)

contentBlocks.original:
सुव्यक्तः सच्चिदानन्दः सुवीरः सुजनाश्रयः ।
सर्वलक्षणसम्पन्नः सत्यधर्मपरायणः ॥ 120 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suvyaktaḥ sachchidānandaḥ suvīraḥ sujanāśrayaḥ ।
sarvalakṣaṇasampannaḥ satyadharmaparāyaṇaḥ ॥ 120 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Suvyakta (completely manifest and clear), Sach-chid-ananda (Existence-Consciousness-Bliss itself), Suvira (the great hero), Sujan-ashray-a (refuge for all good people), Sarva-lakshana-sampanna (endowed with all auspicious marks), Satya-dharma-parayana (supremely devoted to truth and Dharma). (120)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वदेवमयः सत्यः सदा मृष्टान्नदायकः ।
सुधापी सुमतिः सत्यः सर्वविघ्नविनाशनः ॥ 121 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvadēvamayaḥ satyaḥ sadā mṛṣṭānnadāyakaḥ ।
sudhāpī sumatiḥ satyaḥ sarvavighnavināśanaḥ ॥ 121 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sarva-devamaya (composed of all the gods), Satya (truth itself), Sada-mrishtan-na-dayaka (ever-giver of pure and nourishing food), Sudha-pi (drinker of nectar), Sumati (of excellent understanding), Satya (the true one), Sarva-vighna-vinashana (destroyer of all obstacles). (121)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वदुःखप्रशमनः सुकुमारः सुलोचनः ।
सुग्रीवः सुधृतिः सारः सुराराध्यः सुविक्रमः ॥ 122 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvaduḥkhapraśamanaḥ sukumāraḥ sulōchanaḥ ।
sugrīvaḥ sudhṛtiḥ sāraḥ surārādhyaḥ suvikramaḥ ॥ 122 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sarva-duhkha-prashamana (pacifier of all suffering), Sukumara (of tender and delicate form), Sulochana (of beautiful eyes), Sugriva (of beautiful neck and voice), Sudhiti (of excellent resolve and understanding), Sara (the essence of all), Sur-aradhya (to be worshipped by the gods), Suvikrama (of excellent and heroic valour). (122)

contentBlocks.original:
सुरारिघ्नः स्वर्णवर्णः सर्पराजः सदा शुचिः ।
सप्तार्चिर्भूः सुरवरः सर्वायुधविशारदः ॥ 123 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
surārighnaḥ svarṇavarṇaḥ sarparājaḥ sadā śuchiḥ ।
saptārchirbhūḥ suravaraḥ sarvāyudhaviśāradaḥ ॥ 123 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sura-ri-ghna (slayer of the enemies of the gods), Svarna-varna (of golden complexion), Sarpa-raja (king of the serpents), Sada-shuchi (ever pure), Saptarchi-rbhu (earth of the seven flames of fire), Sura-vara (the best and most exalted among the gods), Sarv-ayudha-visharada (proficient in all weapons). (123)

contentBlocks.original:
हस्तिचर्माम्बरसुतो हस्तिवाहनसेवितः ।
हस्तचित्रायुधधरो हृताघो हसिताननः ॥ 124 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hasticharmāmbarasutō hastivāhanasēvitaḥ ।
hastachitrāyudhadharō hṛtāghō hasitānanaḥ ॥ 124 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Hasti-charm-ambara-suta (son of the one who wears an elephant's hide, Shiva), Hasti-vahana-sevita (served by those who ride elephants, served by Indra), Hast-achitra-ayudha-dhara (bearing wonderfully varied weapons in hand), Hrit-agha (remover of the sins of the heart), Hasit-anana (with a smiling, joyful face). (124)

contentBlocks.original:
हेमभूषो हरिद्वर्णो हृष्टिदो हृष्टिवर्धनः ।
हेमाद्रिभिद्धंसरूपो हुङ्कारहतकिल्बिषः ॥ 125 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hēmabhūṣō haridvarṇō hṛṣṭidō hṛṣṭivardhanaḥ ।
hēmādribhiddhaṃsarūpō huṅkārahatakilbiṣaḥ ॥ 125 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Hema-bhusha (adorned with golden ornaments), Hari-dvarna (of greenish-gold complexion), Hristi-da (giver of joy and gladness), Hristi-vardhana (increaser of joy and delight), Hem-adri-bhid-dhamsa-rupa (who has the form of a thunder-cloud cleaving golden mountains), Hunk-ara-hata-kilbisha (whose Hum-syllable has slain all guilt). (125)

contentBlocks.original:
हिमाद्रिजातातनुजो हरिकेशो हिरण्मयः ।
हृद्यो हृष्टो हरिसखो हंसो हंसगतिर्हविः ॥ 126 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
himādrijātātanujō harikēśō hiraṇmayaḥ ।
hṛdyō hṛṣṭō harisakhō haṃsō haṃsagatirhaviḥ ॥ 126 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Himadri-jat-atanuja (son of the daughter of the Himalaya, Parvati), Harikesh-a (of golden or green-hued locks), Hiranmaya (made of gold, golden), Hridya (dwelling in the heart, dear to the heart), Hrishta (ever-joyful and blissful), Hari-sakha (friend of Vishnu/Hari), Hamsa (the divine swan), Hamsa-gati (moving with the grace of the swan), Havis (the oblation, food of the gods). (126)

contentBlocks.original:
हिरण्यवर्णो हितकृद्धर्षदो हेमभूषणः ।
हरप्रियो हितकरो हतपापो हरोद्भवः ॥ 127 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hiraṇyavarṇō hitakṛddharṣadō hēmabhūṣaṇaḥ ।
harapriyō hitakarō hatapāpō harōdbhavaḥ ॥ 127 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Hiranya-varna (of golden colour and hue), Hita-krit (doer of all that is beneficial), Harsha-da (giver of happiness and delight), Hema-bhushana (adorned with golden ornaments), Hara-priya (beloved of Shiva), Hita-kara (doer of good for all), Hata-papa (one in whose presence all sins are destroyed), Hara-udbhava (born of Hara/Shiva). (127)

contentBlocks.original:
क्षेमदः क्षेमकृत्क्षेम्यः क्षेत्रज्ञः क्षामवर्जितः ।
क्षेत्रपालः क्षमाधारः क्षेमक्षेत्रः क्षमाकरः ॥ 128 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣēmadaḥ kṣēmakṛtkṣēmyaḥ kṣētrajñaḥ kṣāmavarjitaḥ ।
kṣētrapālaḥ kṣamādhāraḥ kṣēmakṣētraḥ kṣamākaraḥ ॥ 128 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kshema-da (giver of well-being), Kshema-krit (maker of welfare), Ksheemya (the auspicious one of welfare), Kshetra-jna (knower of the field, the body), Kshama-varjita (beyond all diminishment), Kshetra-pala (protector of the sacred fields and shrines), Kshama-dhara (holder of patience and forbearance), Kshema-kshetra (the field of well-being), Kshama-kara (maker of forgiveness). (128)

contentBlocks.original:
क्षुद्रघ्नः क्षान्तिदः क्षेमः क्षितिभूषः क्षमाश्रयः ।
क्षालिताघः क्षितिधरः क्षीणसंरक्षणक्षमः ॥ 129 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣudraghnaḥ kṣāntidaḥ kṣēmaḥ kṣitibhūṣaḥ kṣamāśrayaḥ ।
kṣālitāghaḥ kṣitidharaḥ kṣīṇasaṃrakṣaṇakṣamaḥ ॥ 129 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kshudra-ghna (destroyer of the petty and the mean), Kshanti-da (giver of patience), Kshema (well-being itself), Kshiti-bhusha (ornament of the earth), Kshama-ashray-a (refuge of the forbearing), Kshalit-agha (one who has washed away all sin), Kshiti-dhara (upholder of the earth), Kshina-samrakshana-ksham-a (capable of protecting even the most weakened). (129)

contentBlocks.original:
क्षणभङ्गुरसन्नद्धघनशोभिकपर्दकः ।
क्षितिभृन्नाथतनयामुखपङ्कजभास्करः ॥ 130 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣaṇabhaṅgurasannaddhaghanaśōbhikapardakaḥ ।
kṣitibhṛnnāthatanayāmukhapaṅkajabhāskaraḥ ॥ 130 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kshana-bhangura-sannaddha-ghana-shobhi-kaparda-ka (whose matted locks are adorned like a glory-cloud, beautiful even in their transience), Kshiti-bhrit-natha-tanaya-mukha-pankaja-bhaskara (the sun that illuminates the lotus-face of the daughter of the lord of mountains, Parvati). (130)

contentBlocks.original:
क्षताहितः क्षरः क्षन्ता क्षतदोषः क्षमानिधिः ।
क्षपिताखिलसन्तापः क्षपानाथसमाननः ॥ 131 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣatāhitaḥ kṣaraḥ kṣantā kṣatadōṣaḥ kṣamānidhiḥ ।
kṣapitākhilasantāpaḥ kṣapānāthasamānanaḥ ॥ 131 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kshat-ahita (destroyer of the foes who wound), Kshara (the flowing, ever-moving one), Kshanta (the patient and forgiving), Kshata-dosha (beyond all flaws), Kshama-nidhi (treasure-house of patience), Kshap-ita-akhila-santapa (one who has ended all suffering), Kshapa-natha-saman-ana (whose face is beautiful as the lord of the night, the moon). (131)

contentBlocks.original:
उत्तर न्यासः ।
करन्यासः –
ॐ शं ओङ्कारस्वरूपाय ओजोधराय ओजस्विने सुहृदयाय हृष्टचित्तात्मने भास्वररूपाय अङ्गुष्ठाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ रं षट्कोणमध्यनिलयाय षट्किरीटधराय श्रीमते षडाधाराय तर्जनीभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ वं षण्मुखाय शरजन्मने शुभलक्षणाय शिखिवाहनाय मध्यमाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ णं कृशानुसम्भवाय कवचिने कुक्कुटध्वजाय अनामिकाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ भं कन्दर्पकोटिदीप्यमानाय द्विषड्बाहवे द्वादशाक्षाय कनिष्ठिकाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ वं खेटधराय खड्गिने शक्तिहस्ताय करतलकरपृष्ठाभ्यां नमः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
uttara nyāsaḥ ।
karanyāsaḥ –
ōṃ śaṃ ōṅkārasvarūpāya ōjōdharāya ōjasvinē suhṛdayāya hṛṣṭachittātmanē bhāsvararūpāya aṅguṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ raṃ ṣaṭkōṇamadhyanilayāya ṣaṭkirīṭadharāya śrīmatē ṣaḍādhārāya tarjanībhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ vaṃ ṣaṇmukhāya śarajanmanē śubhalakṣaṇāya śikhivāhanāya madhyamābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ ṇaṃ kṛśānusambhavāya kavachinē kukkuṭadhvajāya anāmikābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ bhaṃ kandarpakōṭidīpyamānāya dviṣaḍbāhavē dvādaśākṣāya kaniṣṭhikābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ vaṃ khēṭadharāya khaḍginē śaktihastāya karatalakarapṛṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Uttara Nyasa — Closing Ritual: Kara Nyasa (hand-placement): Om Sham — to the form of Om, the radiant one of joyful consciousness — salutations with the thumbs. Om Ram — to the one of the six-pointed centre, the six-crowned, auspicious, six-supported one — salutations with the forefingers. Om Vam — to the six-faced, reed-born, peacock-riding one — salutations with the middle fingers. Om Nam — to the fire-born armoured cock-bannered one — salutations with the ring fingers. Om Bham — to the twelve-armed twelve-eyed one blazing as ten million Kandarpas — salutations with the little fingers. Om Vam — to the shield-, sword-, and spear-bearing one — salutations with the backs and palms of the hands.

contentBlocks.original:
हृदयादिन्यासः
ॐ शं ओङ्कारस्वरूपाय ओजोधराय ओजस्विने सुहृदयाय हृष्टचित्तात्मने भास्वररूपाय हृदयाय नमः ।
ॐ रं षट्कोणमध्यनिलयाय षट्किरीटधराय श्रीमते षडाधाराय शिरसे स्वाहा ।
ॐ वं षण्मुखाय शरजन्मने शुभलक्षणाय शिखिवाहनाय शिखायै वषट् ।
ॐ णं कृशानुसम्भवाय कवचिने कुक्कुटध्वजाय कवचाय हुम् ।
ॐ भं कन्दर्पकोटिदीप्यमानाय द्विषड्बाहवे द्वादशाक्षाय नेत्रत्रयाय वौषट् ।
ॐ वं खेटधराय खड्गिने शक्तिहस्ताय अस्त्राय फट् ।
भूर्भुवस्सुवरोमिति दिग्विमोकः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hṛdayādinyāsaḥ
ōṃ śaṃ ōṅkārasvarūpāya ōjōdharāya ōjasvinē suhṛdayāya hṛṣṭachittātmanē bhāsvararūpāya hṛdayāya namaḥ ।
ōṃ raṃ ṣaṭkōṇamadhyanilayāya ṣaṭkirīṭadharāya śrīmatē ṣaḍādhārāya śirasē svāhā ।
ōṃ vaṃ ṣaṇmukhāya śarajanmanē śubhalakṣaṇāya śikhivāhanāya śikhāyai vaṣaṭ ।
ōṃ ṇaṃ kṛśānusambhavāya kavachinē kukkuṭadhvajāya kavachāya hum ।
ōṃ bhaṃ kandarpakōṭidīpyamānāya dviṣaḍbāhavē dvādaśākṣāya nētratrayāya vauṣaṭ ।
ōṃ vaṃ khēṭadharāya khaḍginē śaktihastāya astrāya phaṭ ।
bhūrbhuvassuvarōmiti digvimōkaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Hridaya Nyasa (body-placement): Om Sham — to the radiant, joyful, Om-formed one — salutations at the heart. Om Ram — to the six-crowned, six-supported one — svaha at the head. Om Vam — to the six-faced, peacock-riding one — vashat at the crown. Om Nam — to the armoured, cock-bannered one — hum as armour. Om Bham — to the twelve-armed, twelve-eyed one — vaushat at the three eyes. Om Vam — to the shield, sword, and spear-bearer — phat as the weapon. Bhur-bhuvas-suvah Om — thus the unbinding of the directions.

contentBlocks.original:
फलश्रुति
इति नाम्नां सहस्राणि षण्मुखस्य च नारद ।
यः पठेच्छृणुयाद्वापि भक्तियुक्तेन चेतसा ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
phalaśruti
iti nāmnāṃ sahasrāṇi ṣaṇmukhasya cha nārada ।
yaḥ paṭhēchChṛṇuyādvāpi bhaktiyuktēna chētasā ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Phala Shruti — the fruits of recitation: Thus, O Narada, these are the thousand names of the six-faced Lord Subrahmanya. Whoever reads or listens to them with a mind full of devotion (1)

contentBlocks.original:
स सद्यो मुच्यते पापैर्मनोवाक्कायसम्भवैः ।
आयुर्वृद्धिकरं पुंसां स्थैर्यवीर्यविवर्धनम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sa sadyō muchyatē pāpairmanōvākkāyasambhavaiḥ ।
āyurvṛddhikaraṃ puṃsāṃ sthairyavīryavivardhanam ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: is immediately freed from all sins born of mind, speech, and body. For men it increases longevity, and builds firm steadiness and vigour. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
वाक्येनैकेन वक्ष्यामि वाञ्छितार्थं प्रयच्छति ।
तस्मात्सर्वात्मना ब्रह्मन्नियमेन जपेत्सुधीः ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vākyēnaikēna vakṣyāmi vāñChitārthaṃ prayacchati ।
tasmātsarvātmanā brahmanniyamēna japētsudhīḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In a single utterance it grants all that is wished for. Therefore, O Brahmin, the wise person should recite it with full discipline and dedication of body, mind, and soul. (3)

contentBlocks.original: इति स्कन्दपुराणे ईश्वरप्रोक्ते ब्रह्मनारदसंवादे श्री सुब्रह्मण्य सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti skandapurāṇē īśvaraprōktē brahmanāradasaṃvādē śrī subrahmaṇya sahasranāma stōtram ।
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Sri Subrahmanya Sahasranama Stotram, as declared by Ishvara, in the dialogue between Brahma and Narada, in the Skanda Purana.
